---
title: "Pediatric Medevac Operations in a Remote Arctic Health System"
subtitle: "Utilization, Timeliness, and Regional Variation"
date: today
date-format: long
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| include: false
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

_proj = Path(os.environ.get("QUARTO_PROJECT_DIR", Path.cwd())).resolve()
# manuscripts/paper1/ → repo root
if (_proj / "analysis").exists():
    _root = _proj
else:
    _root = _proj.parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(_root / "analysis"))

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

from medevac_summaries import (
    load_data,
    count_village_to_mhc_legs,
    filter_journeys_village_to_mhc,
    build_table3a_primary_routes,
    build_table3b_secondary_routes,
    build_table1_village_characteristics,
    build_table2_patient_characteristics_by_age,
    build_table3_village_utilization,
    build_table3_by_year,
    build_timing_category_tables,
    build_table5_timing_minutes,
    plot_fig6_medevacs_per_patient,
    plot_fig1_medevac_activation_map,
    plot_fig_voronoi_service_districts,
    plot_fig1a_monthly_by_village,
    plot_fig1b_monthly_by_age,
    plot_fig2a_annual_by_village,
    plot_fig2b_annual_by_age,
    plot_fig4_sankey_transport_routes,
)

_df_all = load_data()
N_VILLAGE_TO_MHC_LEGS = count_village_to_mhc_legs(_df_all)
df = filter_journeys_village_to_mhc(_df_all)


def show_tbl(d: pd.DataFrame):
    return d.style.hide(axis="index")
```

## Objective

To characterize the structure and performance of pediatric medevac operations across a
geographically dispersed Arctic health system.

**Conceptual framework:** Donabedian (Structure → Process); Rural health access / transport systems.

**Unit of analysis:** Transport leg.

**Target journals:** Prehospital Emergency Care; Air Medical Journal.

---

## Table 1. Medevac characteristics by village

One row per metric, one column per village of origin, sorted by total flights descending.
**Flight time** = activation-to-arrival minutes (measured transport time from medevac activation
to patient arrival at destination). **Utilization rate** = journeys per 1,000 pediatric
residents (2020 Census, Maniilaq service area villages).

```{python}
show_tbl(build_table1_village_characteristics(df))
```

---

## Figure. Primary transports per patient

Distribution of village → MHC transport legs per unique patient across the study period.

```{python}
#| label: fig-transports-per-patient
#| fig-cap: "Number of primary (village → MHC) medevac transport legs per patient."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
_f = plot_fig6_medevacs_per_patient(df, village_to_mhc_only=True)
display(_f)
plt.close(_f)
```

---

## Table 2. Patient characteristics by age group

One row per patient (earliest qualifying journey per MRN). Age groups: <1, 1–<5, 5–12,
13–18 years. n (%) within each age group column. Primary transports per patient shown as
ordered categorical (1, 2, 3+) across all journeys in the study period. **NR\*** = field
not yet present in `pediatric_patients.csv`.

```{python}
show_tbl(build_table2_patient_characteristics_by_age(df))
```

---

## Table 3a. Primary transfers (village → MHC or direct tertiary)

One row per village of origin × destination pair. A **primary leg** is any medevac
leg originating from a village clinic (village → MHC, or village → tertiary center
directly). Percent denominator = all primary legs.

```{python}
show_tbl(build_table3a_primary_routes(_df_all))
```

---

## Table 3b. Secondary transfers (MHC → tertiary center)

One row per tertiary destination. A **secondary leg** originates from Maniilaq Health
Center after an initial village → MHC transport. Percent denominator = all secondary legs.

```{python}
show_tbl(build_table3b_secondary_routes(_df_all))
```

---

## Table 4. Village-level utilization

One row per village of origin, sorted by total legs descending. Utilization rate =
journeys per 1,000 pediatric residents (2020 Census, Maniilaq service area villages).

```{python}
show_tbl(build_table3_village_utilization(df))
```

---

## Table 5. Time intervals

### Categorical timing

```{python}
for col, tbl in build_timing_category_tables(df).items():
    display(Markdown(f"**{col.replace('_', ' ')}**"))
    display(show_tbl(tbl))
```

### Continuous timing (minutes) — village → MHC only

```{python}
show_tbl(build_table5_timing_minutes(df, village_cah_only=True))
```

### Continuous timing (minutes) — all origin types

```{python}
show_tbl(build_table5_timing_minutes(df, village_cah_only=False))
```

---

## Figure 3. Geographic map — flow lines

Village clinic medevac legs to Maniilaq Health Center. Line width scales with leg count;
color with legs per 1,000 pediatric residents under 18 (2020 Census).

```{python}
#| label: fig-map
#| fig-cap: "Village clinic medevac legs to Maniilaq Health Center (Kotzebue). Line width = leg count; color = utilization rate per 1,000 pediatric residents."
#| fig-width: 8.5
#| fig-height: 6.5
#| out-width: 100%
#| fig-align: center
plt.ioff()
plt.close("all")
_map_fig = plot_fig1_medevac_activation_map(_df_all)
display(_map_fig)
plt.close(_map_fig)
```

---

## Figure 3b. Geographic map — service district choropleth

Northwest Arctic Borough divided into approximate medevac service zones (Voronoi
tessellation around village clinic centroids). Each zone is colored by utilization
rate (village→MHC legs per 1,000 pediatric residents, 2020 Census).

```{python}
#| label: fig-map-voronoi
#| fig-cap: "Approximate medevac service districts: Northwest Arctic Borough divided by nearest village clinic. Color = utilization rate per 1,000 pediatric residents."
#| fig-width: 8.5
#| fig-height: 6.5
#| out-width: 100%
#| fig-align: center
plt.ioff()
plt.close("all")
_vor_fig = plot_fig_voronoi_service_districts(_df_all)
display(_vor_fig)
plt.close(_vor_fig)
```

---

## Figure 1A. Monthly medevac volume by village (2020–2024)

```{python}
#| label: fig-1a
#| fig-cap: "Monthly pediatric medevac volume (2020–2024), stacked by village of origin."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 5
#| out-width: 100%
_f = plot_fig1a_monthly_by_village(df)
display(_f)
plt.close(_f)
```

---

## Figure 1B. Monthly medevac volume by age group (2020–2024)

```{python}
#| label: fig-1b
#| fig-cap: "Monthly pediatric medevac volume (2020–2024), stacked by age group."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 5
#| out-width: 100%
_f = plot_fig1b_monthly_by_age(df)
display(_f)
plt.close(_f)
```

---

## Figure 2A. Annual medevac volume by village (2020–2024)

```{python}
#| label: fig-2a
#| fig-cap: "Annual pediatric medevac volume (2020–2024), stacked by village of origin."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| out-width: 100%
_f = plot_fig2a_annual_by_village(df)
display(_f)
plt.close(_f)
```

---

## Figure 2B. Annual medevac volume by age group (2020–2024)

```{python}
#| label: fig-2b
#| fig-cap: "Annual pediatric medevac volume (2020–2024), stacked by age group."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| out-width: 100%
_f = plot_fig2b_annual_by_age(df)
display(_f)
plt.close(_f)
```

---

## Figure 4. Sankey diagram of transport routes

All journeys (not filtered to village→MHC). Flows: Origin Village → Transfer Location 1
(usually Maniilaq Health Center) → Transfer Location 2 → Transfer Location 3.

```{python}
#| label: fig-sankey
#| fig-cap: "Medevac transport routes: village → MHC → tertiary centers. Width of flows proportional to number of journeys."
#| fig-width: 13
#| fig-height: 7
#| out-width: 100%
_f = plot_fig4_sankey_transport_routes(_df_all)
display(_f)
plt.close(_f)
```

---

## Supplemental. Journeys by calendar year

```{python}
show_tbl(build_table3_by_year(df))
```

---

*To render:* `quarto render manuscripts/paper1/paper1.qmd` (from project root).
